raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":0.7,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":0.7,"RX.5":0.7},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":true,"clarification_question":"Do you want sentence-level editing only, or substantive research-writing help covering claims, logic, evidence alignment, and structure?","rationale":{"R1.1":"The request to help with the section can centrally involve reframing the contribution and interpretation claims made at the end of the excerpt.","R1.2":"The section contains an argument chain from benchmark design and results to conclusions about ambiguity signals, making logical consistency a central responsibility.","R1.3":"The delegation reasonably includes checking whether the reported results and metric support the claims drawn from them.","R1.5":"Helping with a whole section can centrally include improving the ordering and narrative arc of the evaluation description and conclusion.","R1.6":"A generic request for help with a section centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and polish.","RX.1":"Any projection should preserve uncertainty about the user's intended kind of help because the delegation is broad.","RX.2":"The artifact contains strong claims about frontier models and solved problems, so avoiding overclaiming is load-bearing.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than editing or critiquing the artifact.","RX.4":"The projection should account for avoiding downstream research or deployment harms from overstated benchmark conclusions.","RX.5":"The projection should keep responsibility tied to the provided delegation and artifact rather than unstated external assumptions."}}

parsed.clarification_question: Do you want sentence-level editing only, or substantive research-writing help covering claims, logic, evidence alignment, and structure?

rationale.R1.1: The request to help with the section can centrally involve reframing the contribution and interpretation claims made at the end of the excerpt.
rationale.R1.2: The section contains an argument chain from benchmark design and results to conclusions about ambiguity signals, making logical consistency a central responsibility.
rationale.R1.3: The delegation reasonably includes checking whether the reported results and metric support the claims drawn from them.
rationale.R1.5: Helping with a whole section can centrally include improving the ordering and narrative arc of the evaluation description and conclusion.
rationale.R1.6: A generic request for help with a section centrally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and polish.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection should preserve uncertainty about the user's intended kind of help because the delegation is broad.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains strong claims about frontier models and solved problems, so avoiding overclaiming is load-bearing.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within projecting responsibilities rather than editing or critiquing the artifact.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should account for avoiding downstream research or deployment harms from overstated benchmark conclusions.
rationale.RX.5: The projection should keep responsibility tied to the provided delegation and artifact rather than unstated external assumptions.
